content: sd block/unblock commands: block.ts uses --by flag; unblock.ts uses --from (specific) or --all (closed blockers). Both maintain bidirectional blocks/blockedBy arrays atomically under lock. Import Command with import type for biome compliance.

sd label command: label.ts uses dep.ts subcommand pattern (add/remove/list/list-all). Labels are free-form strings, normalized lowercase/trimmed on write. Stored as labels?: string[] on Issue — empty arrays become undefined (same as blocks/blockedBy). sd create uses --labels (comma-separated), sd update uses --add-label/--remove-label/--set-labels (comma-separated, applied in order: set→add→remove). sd list filters: --label (AND), --label-any (OR), --unlabeled.

name: sd-list-ready-default-sort
description: sd list and sd ready default to priority asc (P0 first), tie-break by createdAt desc. Override via --sort priority|created|updated|id. Sort logic lives in src/sort.ts (sortIssues, isSortMode, VALID_SORT_MODES) and is shared by both commands.

name: global-format-flag
description: Output-emitting commands (list/ready/show/blocked/stats) accept --format <markdown|compact|plain|ids|json>. --json is preserved as alias for --format json. resolveFormat() in src/format.ts parses both flags; ids mode emits issue IDs one per line for shell pipelines (sd list --format ids | xargs sd close). plain mode strips ANSI via stripAnsi() helper.

content: ANSI-stripping in plain format mode: use stripAnsi() in src/format.ts which builds the regex via String.fromCharCode(27) + new RegExp() to satisfy biome's noControlCharactersInRegex lint rule (a literal /\x1b.../ regex fails lint).

name: shared-issue-filter-helper
description: src/filter.ts hosts applyIssueFilters + filterOptionsFromFlags consumed by both sd list and sd ready. Filters: type, assignee, label (AND), label-any (OR), unlabeled. Add new shared filters here so both commands stay in sync.

name: find-then-narrow-no-bang
description: Replace 'arr[idx]!' after findIndex with the narrow-then-check pattern: 'const issue = issues[idx]; if (!issue) throw...'. TS narrows issue to T after the check, so no non-null assertion needed. Applied across block/close/dep/label/tpl/unblock/update commands.

name: priority-filter-flags
description: sd list and sd ready accept --priority <levels> for exact comma-separated set (e.g. 0,1 or P0,P1) and --priority-max <n> for ceiling (e.g. --priority-max 1 = P0+P1). Both numeric (0-4) and P-prefixed (P0-P4) forms accepted; parsing lives in filter.ts:parsePriorityToken which throws 'Invalid priority "X": must be 0-4 or P0-P4' for invalid tokens. Errors propagate from filterOptionsFromFlags through run() to main().catch in index.ts.

name: sd-search-fulltext
description: sd search <query> performs case-insensitive substring match on title + description; reuses applyIssueFilters for shared filter set (--status/--type/--assignee/--label/--label-any/--unlabeled/--priority/--priority-max) and shared --limit/--sort/--format/--json output flags; includes closed issues by default (unlike list which excludes them) since 'find the one I remember' is the primary use case.

content: src/validation.ts is the SOLE AJV-importing module in seeds (dep boundary). compileSchema(schema) returns ValidatorFn = (data) => {valid:true} | {valid:false, diff:PartialStateDiff}; formatErrors(ajvErrs, submitted) is also exported for callers holding raw AJV errors. Later sd plan submit/validate commands must consume from this module and not re-import ajv.

name: ajv-error-to-partial-state-diff
description: AJV ErrorObject → PLAN_SPEC.md:180-195 PartialStateDiff mapping in src/validation.ts: (1) instancePath slash→dot, leading slash stripped, (2) 'required' errors append params.missingProperty to the path so the entry points at the missing field, (3) keyword passthrough to spec 'code' EXCEPT minItems normalizes to 'min' to match the spec's sections.steps example, (4) per-keyword 'fix' strings via switch — minItems walks the submitted object via instancePath to compute remaining-needed count.

name: plan-command-structure
description: sd plan parent + 6 subcommands all live in src/commands/plan.ts with shared validation (template.validate). Built-in feature template hardcoded in src/plan-templates/feature.ts; AJV schema covers shape, structural blocks-index check (no self-reference, no out-of-range) runs as a second pass since AJV cannot check array-length-relative indices. Plan-awareness for ready/show/list goes through src/plan-context.ts (loadPlanContext + planForIssue + planLineSuffix + summarisePlanChildren) so the integration is DRY.

content: step.blocks: [j] in plan submission uses forward semantics — step i with blocks=[j] means step i blocks step j (step j depends on step i). On submit (PLAN_SPEC.md:248-257), child i.blocks gains child j's id and child j.blockedBy gains child i's id. Bidirectional integrity is wired in src/commands/plan.ts via a unified pass that handles both new and matched children with dedupe.

sd plan submit holds two locks: outer plansPath, inner issuesPath. Build all child issues + parent update + plan row in memory under both locks, then writeIssues (atomic rewrite of parent + new children appended) and appendPlan/writePlans. Parent gets blockedBy=[all children] so sd ready surfaces children, not parent.

src/commands/init.ts must initialize plans.jsonl alongside issues.jsonl/templates.jsonl, and append .seeds/plans.jsonl merge=union to .gitattributes. New stores added to types.ts (constants like PLANS_FILE) need a corresponding init.ts touch.

name: plan-template-config-pipeline
description: Phase 2 plan template pipeline: config.yaml plan_templates: → loadPlanTemplates() → typed PlanTemplate (types.ts) → generatePlanSchema(template) → compilePlanTemplate(template) → AJV ValidatorFn. SOLE config-shape PlanTemplate type lives in types.ts; ALL plan_templates loading flows through config.ts; ALL validation flows through plan-schema.ts. plan.ts only consumes loadPlanTemplates+compilePlanTemplate.

name: plan-lifecycle-state-machine
description: Plan status state machine in src/plan-lifecycle.ts: computeNextPlanStatus(plan, planChildren) returns next status. Rules: draft stays draft (manual). approved->active when any child in_progress. active->done when all children closed. done->active when closed child reopens. active stays active while any non-closed child remains (no regression). Hooked from update.ts (status changes) and close.ts (any close), both under outer plans-lock + inner issues-lock. affectedPlanIds() filters plans by .children intersection. applyPlanTransitions() mutates plans[] in place, returns count changed.

name: plan-overwrite-title-matching
description: sd plan submit --overwrite (PLAN_SPEC.md:374-391): match new steps to existing plan.children by step title (greedy first-match, with usedOldIds Set to handle duplicate titles). Matched children kept verbatim (no rewrite of blockedBy). New steps spawn fresh seed IDs with proper blockedBy/blocks wiring. Obsolete children (in old plan, not matched) emitted to stderr as 'sd close <id> --reason "obsoleted by plan <pl-id> revision <N>"' suggestions; never auto-closed. Parent.blockedBy = (existing blockers NOT in old children) + finalChildIds. plan.revision increments by 1. Plans.jsonl row replaced via writePlans (not append) so git diff shows single-line mutation. Validation failure leaves prior row untouched (validate before lock acquire).

content: Lock acquisition order across seeds commands: outer = plans.jsonl, inner = issues.jsonl. plan-submit, update.ts (when --status changes), close.ts all follow this order. update.ts acquires only issues lock when no status change to keep non-status updates fast.

name: plan-domain-inference
description: src/plan-domain.ts: inferDomain() resolves explicit > labels > file-anchors. Uses Bun.which("ml", { PATH: process.env.PATH }) explicitly because Bun.which without options snapshots PATH at process start, so test PATH mutations are silently ignored. Returns { domain, source } where source is one of explicit|labels|files|none.

name: plan-mulch-shellout
description: src/plan-mulch.ts: enrichPriorArt + recordDecision shell out via Bun.spawnSync. Mulch query JSON shape: { domains: [{ domain, records: [{ id, type, name, description }] }] }. Note actual ml CLI is 'ml --json query <domain> --type <type>' (positional domain, top-level --json), not the spec's 'ml query --domain X --json --limit 5'. No --limit; we slice client-side to 5. ml record decision REQUIRES --title in addition to --rationale, so plan submit passes seed.title as --title.

name: fake-ml-on-path-tests
description: Test pattern for shelling out to fake binaries: mkdtemp/<test>/bin, prepend to process.env.PATH in beforeEach, restore in afterEach. Must call Bun.which('ml', { PATH: process.env.PATH }) in production code so tests can override. To test 'ml absent' branch in subprocess tests, invoke bun via process.execPath (absolute) with PATH set to the empty binDir — separates 'find bun' from 'find ml' on machines where they're co-located.

name: phase4-requires_plan-flag
description: Issue.requires_plan + sd ready exclusion: a child seed with requires_plan=true is hidden from sd ready until its OWN sub-plan reaches approved+. Lookup is by plansBySeed.get(issue.id), not plan_id, because plan_template-spawned children have plan_id=undefined until their sub-plan submit succeeds. Check ordering in src/commands/ready.ts: requires_plan exclusion comes BEFORE the draft-plan-surfacing check so requires_plan children with draft sub-plans stay hidden.

name: phase4-plan_template-spawn
description: Step.plan_template wiring (sd plan submit): when a step declares plan_template, spawn the child with requires_plan=true and leave plan_id UNSET (do not back-link to the parent plan). The back-link is recoverable via plan_step_index + plans.children[]. Submit-time validation rejects plan_template names not present in plan_templates: config. Fresh-submit and overwrite paths both branch on step.plan_template; matched (existing) children during overwrite are kept verbatim regardless.

name: phase4-recursive-plan-show
description: sd plan show recursion (PLAN_SPEC.md:340): root at depth 1, nested levels 2..max_plan_depth (default 3, configurable in .seeds/config.yaml). buildPlanTree returns {plan, children, children_plans[]} where children_plans entries are either nested PlanTreeNodes or {plan_id, truncated:true, hint} stubs. Hint string format is exactly 'depth limit reached — use `sd plan show <pl-id>` to drill in'. Sub-plan lookup uses plansBySeed (most-recent-updated wins on duplicates) since children's plan_id may point to their own sub-plan, not back at the parent. JSON top-level emits children + children_plans in parallel.

content: Config.max_plan_depth: optional field on Config interface in src/types.ts (DEFAULT_MAX_PLAN_DEPTH=3). config.ts exports maxPlanDepth(config) helper. init.ts writes max_plan_depth: 3 explicitly into default config.yaml so the value is visible to users. Read via maxPlanDepth(config), not by direct field access.

name: phase5-plan-outcome-review
description: sd plan outcome and sd plan review subcommands (PLAN_SPEC.md:393-413): outcome holds plansPath+issuesPath locks (validates result enum, reads child statuses for warning); review holds plansPath only (no state transition). Show hint gates on status=approved|active && reviewedBy is null.

name: builtin-plan-templates
description: Built-in plan templates (feature, bug, refactor) live as exported PlanTemplate consts in src/config.ts. loadPlanTemplates spreads BUILTIN_PLAN_TEMPLATES then merges user-declared overrides. plan-schema.ts TYPE_DEFAULTS maps task→feature, bug→bug, feature→feature, epic→feature; refactor is opt-in via --template (no seed type maps to it).

content: src/commands/onboard.ts: bumping ONBOARD_VERSION (e.g. 1→2) requires updating snapshot tests that assert seeds-onboard-v:N — onboard.test.ts has both 'includes version marker' and 'updates outdated section' tests checking the literal.

name: plan-child-backref
description: src/plan-backref.ts: child seeds spawned by sd plan submit carry a description block bookended by <!-- seeds:plan-backref:start --> / <!-- seeds:plan-backref:end --> markers. The block holds step index, plan id, parent seed id+title, template, approach excerpt, and a sd plan show link. applyPlanBackref refreshes only the marker section so manual notes outside the markers (and assignee/labels on the issue row) survive --overwrite.

content: sd plan submit output streams: ✓ success lines and ✓ recorded mulch decision go to stdout (printSuccess, gated by the early-return in --json mode); next-step hints (Next: block) and warnings (⚠ obsolete-child suggestions, ml warnings) go to stderr unconditionally. JSON mode suppresses all stdout human-output and the Next block; stderr warnings still emit. Pattern enforced in src/commands/plan.ts runSubmit.

ml record --json emits structured stdout: {success, command:'record', record: {id: 'mx-xxxxxx', ...}}. Code shelling out to ml record can append --json (positionally tolerant) and JSON.parse stdout to capture the recorded id. Used in src/plan-mulch.ts recordDecision to surface the mx-id back to the user on plan submit success.

name: plan-id-seed-or-pl-resolver
description: src/commands/plan.ts: resolvePlanIdArg(arg, dir) accepts a pl-* plan id or a seed id; for seed ids it reads issues.jsonl unlocked and dereferences seed.plan_id. Used by show/validate/outcome/review to remove the round-trip through 'sd plan list'. Errors: seed exists without plan_id → suggest 'sd plan submit'; otherwise mirror 'Plan not found' phrasing.

content: Issue.extensions?: Record<string, unknown> field is opaque to seeds — store.ts needs no changes because readIssues/writeIssues round-trip via JSON.parse/JSON.stringify, preserving nested objects, arrays, ISO8601 strings, booleans, and null verbatim. Confirms the new-issue-field-checklist (mx-c9a8e4) covers opaque object-typed fields without storage-layer plumbing.

name: extensions-render-line
description: src/output.ts formatExtensionsLine() renders Issue.extensions as 'Extensions: key=value ...' with each value JSON.stringify'd (so strings stay quoted, objects/arrays/null print as JSON literals, round-trippable). Returns null when ext is undefined or has zero own keys, so formatIssueFull omits the line entirely. Wired into formatIssueFull between Labels and description; default + plain (via stripAnsi) modes show it; --format json already serializes the issue so extensions ride along with no extra code. Tested in src/commands/format.test.ts under 'sd show' describe block.

name: extensions-update-shallow-merge
description: src/commands/update.ts: --extensions <json> shallow-merges via {...issue.extensions, ...incoming}; --clear-extensions sets undefined. parseExtensionsPatch rejects arrays/null/scalars (top-level array banned per plan pl-c195 risk #5). Both flags together → 'mutually exclusive' error. Empty merge result drops the field (Object.keys(merged).length===0 → undefined), matching the labels convention. The patch is built before the labels block so combined --extensions + --add-label updates land in one write. Tests: src/commands/update.test.ts (set/merge/overwrite-not-deep/clear/malformed/array/null/scalar/conflict/no-touch/empty-on-undef).

name: extensions-respect-schedule
description: sd ready --respect-schedule (opt-in): excludes issues where Issue.extensions.queued === true (strict equality, not truthy) or extensions.scheduledFor parses via Date.parse() to a future timestamp. Malformed ISO8601 falls through to ready (NaN check). Filter applied after applyIssueFilters and before sort. Default ready (no flag) is unchanged — preserves the seed's risk mitigation #3 (avoid silent loss of queued items).

name: extensions-doctor-check
description: src/commands/doctor.ts checkExtensionsSchema flags Issue.extensions that is not a plain object (rejects null, arrays, scalars). status=warn, fixable=true. fixExtensionsSchema sets issue.extensions=undefined for malformed entries and rewrites the JSONL. The doctor pass-count test in src/commands/doctor.test.ts must increment when a check is added (mx-957e8f convention).

name: sd-config-schema-driven
description: sd config tree (schema/show/set/unset) is the wire surface for warren V2's per-tool config UI: sd config schema emits JSON Schema; sd config set <dot.path> <value> YAML-parses the value, stitches it into a parsed config map, validates the whole post-write file against configSchema() via shared compileSchema, and atomically rewrites .seeds/config.yaml under the file's advisory lock. $schema URI is stripped before AJV compile (compileSchema uses default draft-07; the URI is informational for downstream consumers). Schema additionalProperties:false rejects unknown root keys; SectionSpec required:[required, kind, prompt] means partial field-level writes on a fresh section are rejected — write the full section first, then field-level updates.

content: src/yaml.ts: parseScalarOrFlow is exported (single-line YAML scalar/flow parser). sd config set uses it to YAML-parse CLI <value> args so callers can pass bare scalars (5, true, hello), flow seqs ([1,2,3]), or flow maps ({k: v, ...}) without explicit JSON quoting.

sd config show formatting with --path: object value emits YAML map (no wrapper key); strings print bare; numbers/booleans/arrays print JSON.stringify; --json always wraps in {success, command, path, value} (or {config} for whole-file). Round-trips cleanly with sd config set <path> <value>.

name: sd-show-routes-plan-ids
description: src/commands/show.ts: when the requested id starts with 'pl-' and is not found in issues.jsonl, route to plan.runShow (exported from plan.ts) for markdown/json modes; error with a hint to 'sd plan show' for compact/plain/ids formats. Order matters: try issues first so projects literally named 'pl' (which mint pl-XXXX issue ids) still resolve correctly. Plan id ambiguity is bounded by checking issue lookup first.

name: show-multi-id
description: src/commands/show.ts: sd show <id> [ids...] supports multi-id reads with per-format separators — default human uses a 60-char ─ divider, plain joins blocks with blank lines, compact/ids print one per line, JSON returns {success, command, issues, results, errors?}. Single-id keeps the existing {issue, plan, plan_children} JSON shape and pl-* → sd plan show routing; multi-id treats pl-* as a per-id error. Commander signature 'show <id> [ids...]' mirrors close.ts; argv collection uses a manual loop that skips --format <value> pairs (resolveFormat owns flag parsing). Exit code is non-zero when any id fails but other ids still render.

name: update-clear-close-fields-on-reopen
description: src/commands/update.ts: when --status changes to a non-closed value, the patch sets closedAt=undefined and closeReason=undefined. JSON.stringify drops undefined keys during writeIssues, so the on-disk record loses the stale fields. close metadata only persists when status remains 'closed'.

name: doctor-closed-fields-consistency
description: src/commands/doctor.ts checkClosedFieldsConsistency: status!='closed' with closedAt or closeReason set → fail (--fix clears both); status=='closed' with no closedAt → warn (--fix sets closedAt=updatedAt). Total doctor pass count is now 12 (bumped doctor.test.ts hardcoded count per mx-957e8f).

name: plan-name-field
description: Plan.name?: string (seeds-5640) — short human-readable label for plans, set via sd plan submit --name <text> > plan JSON top-level 'name' > parent seed title (fallback). Surfaced as 'Name:' header in sd plan show, fixed-width truncated column between rev and template in sd plan list (or '(unnamed)' placeholder), and additive field in --json payloads. --overwrite preserves existing name unless --name or plan JSON supplies a new one. normalizePlanName() trims whitespace and treats blank input as 'not provided' so the seed-title fallback kicks in. Tests cover all 6 priority/fallback combinations plus show/list surfacing.

content: new-plan-field-checklist: Adding an optional Plan field end-to-end (e.g. Plan.name in seeds-5640): (1) src/types.ts: add optional field on Plan interface; (2) src/commands/plan.ts SubmittedPlan: add matching optional top-level field so the plan JSON parser sees it; (3) submit CLI gains a flag + action options entry; (4) SubmitOptions: add override field; (5) explicit-value resolution (flag > JSON value) before withLock; seed/existing-plan fallback inside withLock; (6) applyOverwrite gets a name?: arg; only assign when truthy so unset stays absent in JSONL diff; (7) runShow human header: conditional console.log; (8) runList human: fixed-width column; (9) --json needs no changes (plan is spread whole). AJV schema in src/plan-schema.ts is permissive at the root (no additionalProperties:false) so top-level fields beyond template/sections round-trip without schema changes.

step.blocks values in sd plan submit payloads are 1-based step indices (step 1 is the first step, step N is the last). Storage in plans.jsonl, INSTRUCTIONS string, validator (src/plan-schema.ts validateStepBlocks: b===i+1 → self-reference, b<1 || b>len → out-of-range with 'step indices are 1-based; valid range 1..N'), and renderer all align 1-based. Only the internal Issue.plan_step_index back-link on spawned children stays 0-based (it is consumed by code, not authored). Fresh-submit and overwrite wiring in src/commands/plan.ts translate via newChildIds[j-1] / finalChildIds[j-1]. sd plan show renders blocks verbatim (no +1 offset). Historical plans stored before this change are not migrated — stored numbers become slightly off in sd plan show display only; spawned children carry resolved seed-id blocks/blockedBy edges so dependency wiring is unaffected.

Adding an optional field to Step in a plan submission: keep STEP_SCHEMA (src/plan-schema.ts) and SubmittedStep (src/commands/plan.ts) in lockstep — they describe the same wire shape. Schema enforces only shape (type, minLength); existence/business-rule checks (e.g. resolving an existing_seed id) live in runSubmit.

name: plan-submit-adoption-pipeline
description: sd plan submit fresh path supports step.existing_seed adoption via validateAdoptions (pre-write pass that throws on first invalid candidate so the lock callback aborts before writeIssues/writePlans) + a updateChildField closure that dispatches edge writes to allIssues[adoption.seedAllIdx] for adopted seeds or newIssues.find(id) for fresh-spawned. Adopted seeds keep title/type/priority/assignee/status/labels; only plan_id, plan_step_index, description (backref via applyPlanBackref), updatedAt change. The validation pass + writes-at-end shape guarantees atomic rollback: throw inside the inner lock leaves issues.jsonl + plans.jsonl untouched on disk.

name: plan-overwrite-id-precedence
description: sd plan submit --overwrite (PLAN_SPEC.md:387-388, seeds-99ae) matches existing children to new steps with id-first precedence: (1) step.existing_seed (current plan-child or external adoption — validateAdoptions takes allowedCurrentPlanId=existingPlan.id so seeds already in this plan pass the 'no other plan_id' check); (2) step.title against unmatched current plan-children; (3) spawn fresh. External adoptions in overwrite path get linked into the plan in the per-step issue-building loop (set plan_id, plan_step_index, applyPlanBackref), and the parent-blocks edge is added in a follow-up pass after the bidirectional step.blocks wiring. Parent.blockedBy now dedupes against finalChildIds so an external blocker that becomes a plan child doesn't appear twice.

name: plan-adopt-command
description: sd plan adopt <plan-id> <seed-ids...> [--step <i>] (src/commands/plan.ts runAdopt, seeds-2b93): post-submit adoption is link-only — sets plan_id (+ plan_step_index when --step given), prepends seeds:plan-backref block via applyPlanBackref, wires seed.blocks += parent / parent.blockedBy += seed (deduped via appendUnique), appends adopted ids to plan.children, bumps plan.revision once per call. Validation runs in a single pre-write pass over all seed ids (parent-self, not-found, closed, already-attached, duplicate-args, --step out-of-range against plan.sections.steps); first failure throws so issues + plans stay untouched. Lock order outer plansPath / inner issuesPath (mx-f29e43). buildPlanBackref now accepts stepIndex?: number — undefined renders 'Adopted into plan PLAN-ID.' instead of 'Step N of plan PLAN-ID.' Output: stdout JSON {success, command, plan_id, adopted: [], revision} in --json, success lines per adopted seed + revision-bump line otherwise.

name: plan-release-command
description: sd plan release <plan-id> <seed-ids...> (src/commands/plan.ts runRelease, seeds-2b8a): post-submit detach of seeds from a plan; inverse of runAdopt. Each candidate must currently have seed.plan_id === planId. Per seed: strip seeds:plan-backref block via stripPlanBackref (src/plan-backref.ts), clear plan_id + plan_step_index (set to undefined; JSON.stringify drops them, matches mx-8b2e32), drop parent.id from seed.blocks via removeValue helper. On parent: drop released ids from blockedBy. Plan row: drop ids from children, bump revision once per call. Lock order outer plans / inner issues (mx-f29e43). Pre-write validation pass — closed seeds, mismatched plan_id, parent seed, duplicate ids, unknown plan all reject before any writes (atomic batch). Release does NOT close the seed; status stays as-is. JSON shape: {success, command:'plan release', plan_id, released: string[], revision}.

name: plan-backref-strip
description: stripPlanBackref(existing: string | undefined) → string | undefined (src/plan-backref.ts, seeds-2b8a): inverse of applyPlanBackref. Locates seeds:plan-backref:start...end markers and slices the block, collapsing surrounding whitespace on both sides. Returns the original input unchanged when no markers are present; returns undefined when only the block remained (lets callers drop the description field). Manual notes positioned before, after, or surrounding the block are preserved. Used by sd plan release to detach seeds without losing author content.

name: plan-adopted-children-field
description: Plan.adoptedChildren?: string[] is the subset of plan.children that were attached via adoption (existing_seed at submit, or sd plan adopt post-submit) rather than fresh-spawned. Lifecycle: runSubmit fresh path sets it from validateAdoptions output (only when non-empty); applyOverwrite preserves entries for survivors (filter by finalChildIds) and merges adoptedExternalIds; runAdopt appends; runRelease filters out and deletes the field when empty. summarisePlanChildren reads plan.adoptedChildren to set ChildSummary.adopted; rendered as ' (adopted)' tag by runShow children list, renderNestedPlanHuman, and src/commands/show.ts renderPlanBlock. Always drop the field when empty so plans without adoption stay byte-identical in JSONL.

name: prime-structured-sections
description: sd prime --json emits typed 'sections' alongside 'content' (seeds-e445). Structured data in src/commands/prime.ts (FULL_SECTIONS, COMPACT_SECTIONS) is the source of truth; renderFull/renderCompact derive the markdown. Full-mode sections: { mode, title, contextRecovery, closeProtocol: { warning, steps, footer }, rules, commandGroups: [{ name, commands: [{ command, description }], notes? }], workflows: [{ name, commands }] }. Compact-mode: { mode, title, commands, planningNote, closingNote }. When a custom .seeds/PRIME.md overrides defaults, sections is null. Non-JSON output is byte-identical to the legacy template — existing prime tests are unchanged.

name: pi-extension scaffolding mirrors mulch shape
description: Seeds ships its in-tree pi-coding-agent extension at extensions/pi/index.ts with a parallel lib/config.ts, peerDependencies + peerDependenciesMeta.optional for @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent and typebox, a pi.extensions manifest entry in package.json, and the pi-package keyword. The pi.* knobs live inside .seeds/config.yaml (file-locking, AJV validation, atomic writes inherited) and are surfaced via configSchema(). sd setup is added as the install surface, modeled after ml setup: a ProviderRecipe { install, check, remove } registry with --list / --check / --remove / --json dispatch. Adding pi-coding-agent as a dev dep pulls in @anthropic-ai/sdk + @google/genai whose .d.ts files require skipLibCheck:true in tsconfig.json. tsconfig include and biome files.includes must add 'extensions/**'.

name: pi-seeds-slash-commands
description: extensions/pi/lib/commands.ts: /sd, /sd:ready, /sd:create, /sd:show, /sd:close, /sd:claim shell out to sd <cmd> --json via pi.exec; results are injected as steer messages (display: false). /sd:claim pins 'working: <id>' onto the status widget and persists via pi.appendEntry('seeds-current-issue', {id}); collectPersistedCurrentIssueId() walks sessionManager.getEntries() on session_start to rehydrate. /sd:close clears the prefix when closing the active issue. All commands registered once at load via buildCommandRegistrations(getDeps) — handlers are inert when getDeps() returns undefined (between sessions, seeds not initialized, or pi.commands=false).

name: setup-recipe-flips-onboard-variant-via-marker-suffix
description: When adding a `sd setup <provider>` recipe that ships an alternate CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md snippet, encode the variant as a suffix on the existing onboarding schema marker (`<!-- seeds-onboard-schema:N:<provider> -->`) rather than adding a parallel marker scheme. Pair with an exported `runOnboard({ variant, silent })` from onboard.ts so the recipe can flip the snippet without double-logging, and an `isPi`-style detector that reads the provider's settings file so a bare `sd onboard` re-run keeps the variant. Detection logic stays one branch in `detectStatus(content, variant)`: marker section present + exact suffix match = current; mismatch = outdated. Mirrors mulch's pl-5563 / `ml setup pi` shape so the two extensions feel like siblings.

name: plan-submit-title-optional-on-adopt
description: STEP_SCHEMA no longer requires step.title; the title-or-existing_seed invariant is enforced by validateStepTitleOrAdopt in src/plan-schema.ts (post-AJV pass alongside validateStepBlocks). Adoption-only submits (warren §11.Q synthesis) can pass steps as plain {existing_seed: <id>} with no titles. validateAdoptions label and the title-mismatch warning in src/commands/plan.ts both branch on step.title presence. plans.jsonl children projection stays byte-compatible.

name: outputJson-async-bun-write
description: outputJson in src/output.ts is async and uses await Bun.write(Bun.stdout, ...) instead of console.log to prevent truncation when stdout is piped. All ~66 call sites across 24 command files use await. Direct Bun.write(Bun.stdout, ...) calls in src/index.ts (error handler) and src/commands/config.ts (schema/value display) follow the same pattern.

name: plan-edit-name
description: sd plan edit <id> --name <text> (seeds-9b12 / pl-dee8 step 1, src/commands/plan.ts runEdit): in-place plan field editing scaffolded for V1 with --name only; --section and --step land in sibling steps. Resolves plan id via resolvePlanIdArg so seed ids work too; holds only the outer plans lock (no issues mutation yet — that arrives with --section approach backref refresh and --step child propagation). Always bumps plan.revision and updatedAt, even when value is unchanged — revision bump is the contract callers rely on for cache invalidation. normalizePlanName is reused for trimming; empty/whitespace --name is rejected (vs treated as 'unset') because plan.name is currently never cleared in edit. Throws 'No fields to edit' when zero flags supplied so commander's accept-all-optional posture doesn't silently bump revision.

name: plan-edit-step
description: sd plan edit <id> --step <i> --title/--priority/--type (seeds-64cf / pl-dee8 step 3, src/commands/plan.ts runEdit + parseStepPatch): in-place step metadata edits. parseStepPatch enforces --step required when any of --title/--priority/--type given, and vice versa; --step is 1-based on CLI (mx-cf60e9), stored 0-based internally. Validates step index against plan.sections.steps.length. Updates plan.sections.steps[i] (shallow merge preserving other fields like blocks/plan_template), then propagates title/priority/type to ALL children where plan_step_index === i (handles --step adoption where multiple seeds share an index). Shares one issues lock with --section approach backref refresh so combined --section + --step edits stay atomic. JSON output adds propagated_children: string[].

name: plan-edit-docs-surface
description: Documenting sd plan edit ships across six surfaces in one go: README 'Editing a plan in place' subsection + Plan Commands table row, PLAN_SPEC.md 'In-Place Editing' section + command-surface block, CLAUDE.md Plan Commands fence + Planning notes + auto-rendered seeds-onboard block (re-run 'bun src/index.ts onboard' to sync after editing onboard.ts), src/commands/prime.ts Planning command group, src/commands/onboard.ts planning bullets (bump ONBOARD_SCHEMA + onboard.test.ts schema assertions AND setup.test.ts schema assertions — both files hardcode the integer). Schema version 5→6 means three test files needed the bump: onboard.test.ts (4 sites) + setup.test.ts (6 sites covering :pi variant). Lock order/atomicity language reused from existing adopt/release/outcome/review docs keeps the planning surface consistent.

title: Optimize test suite: eliminate subprocess spawning bottleneck
rationale: Build a lightweight in-process test harness that captures console.log/console.error/process.stdout.write/Bun.write(Bun.stdout) output, then convert command tests to call the exported run() functions directly instead of spawning subprocesses. Commands already export run(args, seedsDir?) — no production code refactor needed. A small set of CLI smoke tests (5-10) remain subprocess-based to verify the commander wiring, --help, --version, and exit codes. The conversion is mechanical and file-by-file, so each step is independently shippable and testable.

name: in-process-test-harness
description: src/test-harness.ts (runCli, runCliJson) captures stdout/stderr/exitCode for command tests in-process, avoiding bun-spawn overhead. Captures all four output channels: console.{log,info,warn,error}, process.{stdout,stderr}.write, and Bun.write(Bun.stdout|Bun.stderr) — Bun.write passthrough preserves file IO. Chdirs into cwd and restores process.cwd/exitCode/console/streams in finally. Forces chalk.level=0 at module load so output matches subprocess (pipe) baseline. Dispatch: prefers mod.run(args.slice(1), seedsDir); falls back to building a fresh commander program and parseAsync(['bun','sd',cmd,...]) for commands that only export register() (plan, config, setup, completions). Bun test runs files serially in one process by default, so chdir/console-swap is safe; do not use --concurrent with harness-based tests.

Commander's exitOverride() does NOT propagate to subcommands automatically — sub-Commands keep the default _exit (process.exit). Under the in-process test harness (src/test-harness.ts), invoking 'sd <cmd> --help' for any cmd registered via mod.register(program) would call process.exit(0) when the sub's --help fired, silently killing the entire bun:test runner mid-suite. Symptom: bun test src/commands/plan.test.ts exits 0 with no reporter output after one of the --help tests runs; subset filters that avoid --help tests pass fine; output truncated at the first --help test executed.
resolution: After mod.register(program), walk program.commands recursively and call exitOverride() on every subcommand. Also extend the harness catch to treat CommanderError.code === 'commander.helpDisplayed' / 'commander.version' as exitCode 0 (matches subprocess behavior). src/test-harness.ts now does both. Result: plan.test.ts 127s → 9s, full suite still 875 pass.

content: Converting a *.test.ts from subprocess to in-process via src/test-harness.ts is a one-line swap when no test reads stdin: keep the local 'run(args, cwd)' helper signature and delegate to runCli. Tests that exercise '--plan -' (Bun.stdin.text in plan.ts) must stay on Bun.spawn — Bun.stdin.text() hangs under the harness. See src/commands/plan.test.ts: top-level run wraps runCli, single stdin test keeps an inline Bun.spawn block. Result for plan.test.ts: 127s → 9s.

In-process test-harness limits (seeds-c709): the harness builds a fresh commander program registered with ONLY the current command, so tests asserting top-level CLI plumbing (sd --help, sd completions script, sd <unknown> typo suggestions, sd <cmd> --timing stderr emitted by the top-level preAction hook) MUST stay subprocess-based. Three additional in-process pitfalls: (1) search.ts exports its own run() with custom arg parsing, so the harness skips commander's 'missing required argument' error and emits 'Usage: sd search ...' instead — adjust the test assertion, not the command; (2) sync.ts's worktree guard fires only when seedsDir is undefined, but runCli() always passes join(cwd,'.seeds'), so the worktree-branch test stays subprocess; (3) completion-script generation enumerates program.commands which is stubbed to just [completions] in the harness — keep subprocess. Document each subprocess hold-out with an inline comment.

name: cli-smoke-subprocess-coverage
description: src/cli-smoke.test.ts (seeds-d1ca / pl-86aa step 5): the only test file that intentionally spawns 'bun run src/index.ts' as a subprocess. Covers surfaces the in-process harness (src/test-harness.ts) cannot reach: top-level commander program boot via registerAll(), the custom root --help renderer in src/index.ts, the early --version --json short-circuit, the unknown-command branch (typo suggester + exit 1), and the main().catch JSON error wrapper. Keep this file SMALL (~7 tests); each spawn costs ~250ms. Anything testable in-process belongs in the command's own *.test.ts via runCli/runCliJson, not here. Always set env.NO_COLOR=1 so chalk-detected pipe output is stable across CI.

name: test-suite-perf-baseline
description: After pl-86aa, full bun test runs ~882 tests in ~18s (down from ~99s, 5.5x). Acceptance target was <15s; 18s reflects unavoidable parallelism gating on the 5 plan-submit-record-decision tests (~600ms each via fake ml binary + 2 sd subprocesses) and CLI-feature tests (cli-smoke, completions, suggestions, timing) that must remain subprocess. When this baseline regresses by >25%, suspect: (a) a command test reverted from runCli to Bun.spawn, (b) a new subprocess-based test file got added outside the justified set, or (c) bun version regressed module-resolution speed. Per-file timing: gawk over 'bun test' output, summing [Xms] markers grouped by '^(src|extensions).*\.test\.ts:'.

name: plan-step-labels-schema
description: STEP_SCHEMA in src/plan-schema.ts and SubmittedStep in src/commands/plan.ts both carry an optional labels?: string[] (seeds-7561 / pl-e5a8 step 1). Schema enforces array of trimmed non-empty strings via minLength:1 + pattern '\\S'; normalization (lowercase/trim/dedup) and propagation into spawned children/adoption paths land in follow-up steps seeds-745e (fresh spawn) and seeds-bac9 (adoption). Mirrors the optional-step-field pattern documented in mx-4ade46.

name: plan-step-labels-adoption-merge
description: Adoption paths (submit-time fresh + overwrite, runAdopt post-submit) merge step.labels additively into the adopted seed's existing labels via mergeAdoptedLabels() in src/commands/plan.ts (seeds-bac9 / pl-e5a8 step 3). Helper normalizes via normalizeLabels (lowercase/trim/dedup), unions with seed.labels, never strips. runAdopt only merges when --step <i> is given (reads plan.sections.steps[i].labels). Adoption stays link-only otherwise; user-added labels survive plan submits and overwrite revisions.

name: plan-step-labels-test-matrix
description: src/commands/plan.test.ts 'sd plan: step.labels' covers all five paths the field flows through: STEP_SCHEMA validation (empty/whitespace/non-array rejected; empty-array accepted), fresh-spawn (normalize+dedup, lowercase+trim, omit when missing), submit-time existing_seed adoption (additive merge with manual labels, no-op when missing, dedup when overlapping), --overwrite (matched-child additive merge, newly-spawned application, external-adoption merge, no-strip when v2 drops field), and runAdopt --step <i> (pulls from blueprint.steps[i-1].labels, no-op without --step). Pattern: feed plan.sections.steps[k].labels via a cast to Record<string, unknown> when validPlanFor()'s typed shape doesn't include the field.

name: plan-step-labels-docs-surface
description: Documenting Plan step.labels lands across six surfaces in one go: (1) INSTRUCTIONS const in src/commands/plan.ts (sd plan prompt agent-facing instructions) — describe step shape and labels normalization; (2) plan submit addHelpText example in src/commands/plan.ts — include labels in the sample steps array; (3) README submission example + prose; (4) PLAN_SPEC.md 'Section kind reference' steps row; (5) src/commands/prime.ts Planning command group notes (loaded by sd prime); (6) CLAUDE.md Planning bullets. CHANGELOG [Unreleased] Added gets a single bullet citing the parent seed and plan id. Mirrors mx-c86ccf (plan-edit-docs-surface) and mx-8df8eb (sd plan subcommand docs).

name: plan-adopt-only-create-reorder
description: seeds-3dd1: sd plan create <seed> writes an adopt-only plan (children:[], sections:{steps:[]}, status approved, parent seed.plan_id set) so a release train assembles existing seeds with no junk children. sd plan adopt gains --at/--before/--after (mutually exclusive; parseAdoptPosition + resolveInsertIndex in src/commands/plan.ts) to insert the adopted batch at a position in plan.children. sd plan reorder <plan> <seed-ids...> sets the exact children order (must be a permutation; plans.jsonl lock only, no issue mutation). warren plan-run consumes plan.children order verbatim (seq=index+1).

content: When plan.ts crosses the check:size budget (scripts/file-size-budgets.json), extract a cohesive block into a new module rather than raising the budget (ratchet only goes down). The plan-show render/tree helpers + runShow + resolvePlanIdArg moved to src/commands/plan-show.ts; plan.ts re-imports runShow/resolvePlanIdArg and src/commands/show.ts imports runShow from there. New files needing the relaxed cognitive-complexity rule must be added to the includes[] list in biome.json's overrides (e.g. plan-show.ts alongside plan.ts).
